title: Kalmiella hirsuta
intro: Kalmiella hirsuta, also known as hairy wintergreen, is a rare and endemic plant species found in the Western Ghats of India. It is valued for its medicinal properties and has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Kalmiella hirsuta include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Kalmiella hirsuta is commonly prepared as a decoction or a tea, and its extracts are also used in the production of herbal supplements and traditional medicines.
